title.en: TREATMENT OF INORGANIC MATERIALS, OTHER THAN FIBROUS FILLERS, TO ENHANCE THEIR PIGMENTING OR FILLING PROPERTIES  (treatment of materials specially adapted to enhance their filling properties in mortars, concrete or artificial stone C04B14/00, C04B18/00, C04B20/00); PREPARATION OF CARBON BLACK  {; PREPARATION OF INORGANIC MATERIALS WHICH ARE NO SINGLE CHEMICAL COMPOUNDS AND WHICH ARE MAINLY USED AS PIGMENTS OR FILLERS}
Treatment of specific inorganic materials other than fibrous fillers (tenebrescent materials C09K9/00; luminescent materials C09K11/00); Preparation of carbon black
{Compounds of molybdenum (C09C1/0015 takes precedence)}
{containing bismuth and vanadium (C09C1/0015 takes precedence)}
{Pigments for ceramics (C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
{Pigments exhibiting interference colours, e.g. transparent platelets of appropriate thinness or flaky substrates, e.g. mica, bearing appropriate thin transparent coatings}
{Pigments consisting of flaky, non-metallic substrates, characterised by a surface-region containing free metal}
{Composite particulate pigments or fillers, i.e. containing at least two solid phases, except those consisting of coated particles of one compound (C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
{Compounds of antimony (C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of alkaline earth metals or magnesium {(C09C1/0003, C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence; dolomitic solids C09C1/009)}
Compounds of zinc {(C09C1/0003, C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of cadmium {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of lead {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of iron {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of silicon {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of chromium {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078, C09C1/08, C09C1/20 take precedence)}
Compounds of titanium {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of mercury {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078 take precedence)}
Compounds of aluminium {(C09C1/0009, C09C1/0015, C09C1/0078, C09C1/32 take precedence)}
Carbon
Metallic pigments or fillers {(C09C1/0015 takes precedence)}
Copper alloys, e.g. bronze
Treatment in general of inorganic materials, other than fibrous fillers, to enhance their pigmenting or filling properties
{Flushing}
{Combinations of treatments provided for in groups C09C3/04 - C09C3/12}
Physical treatment, e.g. grinding or treatment with ultrasonic vibrations {(C09C3/006 takes precedence)}
Treatment with inorganic compounds {(C09C3/006, C09C3/048 take precedence)}
Treatment with low-molecular-weight {non-polymer} organic compounds {(C09C3/006, C09C3/048 take precedence)}
Treatment with macromolecular organic compounds {(C09C3/006 takes precedence)}
Treatment with organosilicon compounds {(C09C3/006 takes precedence)}
Compositional and structural details of pigments exhibiting interference colours
Interference pigments characterized by the core material
Interference pigments comprising a layer with a concentration gradient or a gradient of the refractive index
Interference pigments comprising a metallic reflector or absorber layer, which is not adjacent to the core
Interference pigments characterised by the thickness of the core or layers thereon or by the total thickness of the final pigment particle
Interference pigments comprising an outermost surface coating
Interference pigments comprising a layer or a core consisting of or comprising discrete particles, e.g. nanometric or submicrometer-sized particles
Special effects or uses of interference pigments
Optical properties in the IR-range, e.g. camouflage pigments
Optical properties in the UV-range
A layer or the substrate forming a grating
Embossed layers
Fluorescent, luminescent or photoluminescent properties
Interference with laser-light, laser markable pigments
Methods of preparing the interference pigments
Wet methods, e.g. co-precipitation
PVD, CVD methods or coating in a gas-phase using a fluidized bed
